summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #720 (selection-agnostic epic audit) by fixing two P2 bugs in SegmentGroup controlled-value sync and TagsInput proptest generator, then re-trigger review.
summary.outcome: Both fixes were implemented, tested, and committed as a follow-up commit on the branch. The SegmentGroup now validates controlled values against incoming item props and per-item disabled state; the TagsInput proptest generator no longer emits controlled values that violate max/duplicate invariants. The commit was staged and pushed but the PR re-trigger was not yet confirmed in the transcript.

repo: SegmentGroup's SetValue handler was using stale registered-item ordering rather than the item collection reflected by incoming SetProps, causing controlled values to bypass disabled-state and item-existence checks.

The TagsInput proptest had an #[ignored] test (tags_input_preserves_invariants) because the controlled-value generator could emit values (e.g. duplicates or length > max) that violated the component's own invariants before the component even acted on them.
The repo enforces a co-authored-by trailer for Codex-originated commits: 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>'.
Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a top-level PR comment; completion is signaled by removing the eyes reaction and posting inline threads.

code.finding: sync_props_plan (around this range) did not apply SetProps before SetValue when both arrive together, meaning newly-introduced items were invisible during controlled-value validation.
Controlled-value acceptance checked whole-group disabled state instead of per-item disabled state, allowing disabled items to be selected via SetValue when the group itself was enabled.
The controlled-value strategy now generates only unique lists with length <= min(generated_max, 1) so every generated SetValue is valid under every generated max, eliminating the false-failure class.

workflow: Codex review polling uses a ~2-minute interval; eyes reaction = reviewing, absence of eyes + threads = finished. Stall threshold is separate from the normal review window.
Reproducing a proptest failure before patching the generator is the correct order: confirm the failure mode is real, then constrain the generator to exclude the invalid input class.
cargo +nightly fmt --check should be run before committing; a single formatting diff blocked a clean commit here.
The review-loop skill (SKILL.md) governs the full @codex review → poll → address → re-trigger cycle and should be read before starting any Codex review loop.

friction: Codex review took 3+ polling cycles (~6+ minutes) before producing findings, requiring repeated sleep/poll loops with no intermediate feedback.
The llvm-cov run was still in-progress when the session was interrupted, so coverage results were not confirmed.
The session was interrupted mid-flow (after committing but before pushing and re-triggering review), requiring re-orientation on resume.
open_items: Confirm the follow-up commit was pushed to origin and the PR branch is up to date.
Post inline replies to Codex's two P2 threads referencing the fix commit, then re-trigger '@codex review'.
Confirm llvm-cov results for selection::segment_group and selection::tags_input after the coverage run completes.
The SegmentGroup regression tests were added for three specific cases; broader prop-sync ordering scenarios may warrant additional coverage in future.
